response.justification: The candidate is a web and full-stack software developer with no discernible experience in SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or deep-learning hardware optimization — the core competencies this role demands. Their closest proximity to hardware is C/C++ work for a measurement IDE and embedded/robotic-arm tooling, neither of which involves AI accelerator design or performance architecture. AI involvement is limited to agentic software frameworks (MCP), not hardware-level workload analysis. Even with a Bachelor's degree satisfying the education path, the experience profile is misaligned in domain.
strengths: Broad multi-language engineering background including low-level C/C++ and DLang for performance-sensitive applications
Demonstrated interest in performance: low-latency microservices with async I/O, caching, and high-performance databases
AI-adjacent work at the software layer (MCP tooling, agentic workflow infrastructure) shows awareness of the AI ecosystem
concerns: Zero experience with SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture, or hardware-software co-design for AI accelerators
No background in deep neural network training or inference optimization at any level (hardware or software)
Career trajectory is firmly web/full-stack; there is no evidence of movement toward hardware architecture or performance engineering at the chip level

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP or GPU architecture experience
Some low-level C/C++ and performance-oriented coding
